label: Title
verbatim: AA-SVD: Anchored and Adaptive SVD for Large Language Model Compression
paraphrase: Paper title — AA-SVD = Anchored and Adaptive SVD (note: spec sometimes calls it 'Anchored Adaptive'; the paper expands the second 'A' as 'Adaptive').
consequences: Disambiguates the AA acronym used by the spec (D8).

label: Abstract
verbatim: By anchoring each compressed layer to the original outputs while explicitly modeling input distribution shifts, our method finds a low-rank approximation that maintains functional equivalence with the original model.
paraphrase: Core idea: compressed layer must (a) reproduce the *original* (uncompressed) target output and (b) operate on the *shifted* input distribution that arises after upstream compression. This is the dual-anchoring principle the spec refers to as ε*-style activation weighting (D8).
preconditions: Sequential block-wise compression with calibration set.
consequences: Distinguishes AA-SVD from input-aware (X only) and shift-aware (X' only) baselines.

label: Figure 2 (left)
verbatim: 1 Input-agnostic: ‖W − W'‖²_F ... 2 Input-aware: ‖WX − W'X‖²_F ... 3 Shift-aware: ‖WX' − W'X'‖²_F ... 4 Anchored adaptive (ours): ‖WX − W'X'‖²_F — anchors the target to the original output while conditioning on the shifted input.
paraphrase: Taxonomy of four layer-wise objectives. AA-SVD adopts objective (4): target = original output WX (anchor), regressor input = shifted X' (adaptive).
preconditions: X = activations from original model; X' = activations from partially compressed model on same calibration data.
consequences: This is the canonical AA-SVD layer objective referenced by Stage 3 of the spec.

label: Fig.2 closed-form
paraphrase: Closed-form AA-SVD solution shown in Figure 2: whiten via L_{X'}, project cross-covariance W·X·X'^T·(X'X'^T)^{-1}·L_{X'} into whitened space, truncate to rank k via SVD, then de-whiten by L_{X'}^{-1}.
preconditions: X' X'^T invertible; L_{X'} satisfies X' X'^T = L_{X'} L_{X'}^T (Cholesky or EVD).
consequences: Concrete formula Stage 3 must implement; involves both the original-input covariance (X X'^T cross term) and the shifted-input covariance (X' X'^T).

label: Fig.2 block loss
paraphrase: Block-level refinement objective: original block on original inputs vs. compressed block on shifted inputs (same anchored-adaptive spirit at block granularity).
preconditions: Per-block, with all upstream blocks already compressed and frozen.
consequences: Defines the Stage-3 block-level fine-tune target.

label: §3 intro
verbatim: a faithfully compressed layer must simultaneously satisfy two constraints: its outputs should remain close to those of the uncompressed model, and it must operate correctly on the inputs it will actually receive at inference time
paraphrase: Central justification of AA-SVD: dual anchoring (output-anchored, input-adaptive).
consequences: Motivates the ‖WX − W'X'‖²_F objective.

label: §3 intro
verbatim: errors across the multiple linear layers within a transformer block can interact, so that even small per-layer errors compound into a larger distortion at the block output. We therefore introduce a block-level refinement step
paraphrase: Per-layer compression alone is insufficient — block-level joint refinement is required to let layers compensate for each other.
preconditions: Multiple linear layers per block (Q/K/V/O for attn, gate/up/down for MLP).
consequences: Block-level refinement is positioned (and later confirmed in §4.3) as the dominant quality factor.

verbatim: f' = W' is a low-rank matrix with rank(W') = k ≤ min(m, n), decomposed as W' = UV^T with U ∈ R^{m×k} and V ∈ R^{n×k}
paraphrase: Compressed weight stored as two thin factors U (m×k) and V (n×k); rank k bounded by min(m,n).
consequences: Sets storage layout used by Algorithm 1's return.

label: Lemma 3.1
verbatim: min_{rank(W')≤k} ‖W − W'‖²_F = Σ_{i>k} σ_i(W)², and the unique minimizer is W'⋆ = SVD_k(W) = U_k Σ_k V_k^T
paraphrase: Eckart–Young–Mirsky: best rank-k Frobenius approximation is truncated SVD; residual energy is the tail singular values.
preconditions: W ∈ R^{m×n} with thin SVD W = UΣV^T.
consequences: Foundation invoked by Theorem 3.2's reduction.

label: Theorem 3.2 (Eq. 1)
verbatim: min_{rank(W')≤k} ‖W A − W' B‖²_F
paraphrase: General two-matrix low-rank regression: target W A, regressor B, find best rank-k W'.
preconditions: W fixed; A, B ∈ R^{n×l}; BB^T invertible; target rank k ∈ N.
consequences: Generalizes input-aware (A=B=X), shift-aware (A=B=X') and anchored-adaptive (A=X, B=X') as special cases.

label: Theorem 3.2 solution
verbatim: W'⋆ = SVD_k( W A B^T (B B^T)^{-1} L_B ) L_B^{-1}
paraphrase: Closed-form optimum: form M = W A B^T (B B^T)^{-1} L_B, take rank-k truncated SVD, then right-multiply by L_B^{-1}. Here L_B is any invertible square root of BB^T (Cholesky or QΛ^{1/2}).
preconditions: BB^T = L_B L_B^T invertible; otherwise use Moore–Penrose (BB^T)^{+1/2} or Tikhonov regularization (Remark in §A.1).
consequences: This is THE formula Stage 3 of the spec must implement when activation-weighting via the AA-SVD ε*-style construction.

label: Theorem 3.2 proof
verbatim: ‖W A − W' B‖²_F = tr(W' B B^T W'^T) − 2 tr(W A B^T W'^T) + ‖W A‖²_F ... ‖W' L_B − W A B^T L_B^{-T}‖²_F − ‖W A B^T L_B^{-T}‖²_F + ‖W A‖²_F
paraphrase: Proof reduces the objective to ‖W' L_B − M‖²_F with M = W A B^T (BB^T)^{-1} L_B = W A B^T L_B^{-T}; then EYM gives W' L_B = SVD_k(M), so W'⋆ = SVD_k(M) L_B^{-1}.
consequences: Confirms the whitening interpretation used by Algorithm 1.

label: Theorem 3.2 Remark
verbatim: If BB^T is singular, ... replace L_B^{-1} by the Moore–Penrose factor (BB^T)^{+1/2}, or equivalently use a Tikhonov-regularized factorization BB^T + εI = L_ε L_ε^T and let ε → 0+.
paraphrase: Numerical safety: when shifted-input covariance is rank-deficient, regularize with εI (Tikhonov) or use pseudo-inverse square root.
consequences: Justifies adding a small ridge ε to the covariance during Stage 3 implementation.

label: Corollary 3.3
verbatim: If B = A, then A B^T = B B^T = L_B L_B^T, so M = W L_B^T. The solution reduces to W'⋆ = SVD_k(W L_B) L_B^{-1}, the standard whitening-based low-rank regression solution.
paraphrase: When the input-aware (A=B=X) or shift-aware (A=B=X') case applies, Theorem 3.2 collapses to the classic SVD-LLM whitening formula W'⋆ = SVD_k(W L_X) L_X^{-1}.
consequences: Recovers SVD-LLM (B=X) and Dobi-SVD (B=X') as special cases of the same closed form.

label: §A.2
verbatim: SVD-LLM (Wang et al., 2025c) and SVD-LLM V2 (Wang et al., 2025b) both correspond to the B = X case, differing only in their factorization of XX^T: SVD-LLM uses the lower-triangular Cholesky factor L_X, while SVD-LLM V2 uses the eigendecomposition XX^T = QΛQ^T with L_X = QΛ^{1/2}, giving W'⋆ = SVD_k(W Q Λ^{1/2}) Λ^{-1/2} Q^T.
paraphrase: SVD-LLM ↔ Cholesky factor of XX^T; SVD-LLM V2 ↔ eigendecomposition factor. Both reduce to Corollary 3.3 with B=X.
consequences: Spec's reference to SVD-LLM whitening is exactly Corollary 3.3 with Cholesky.

label: §3.2 objective
verbatim: min_{W' : rank(W')=k} ‖W X − W' X'‖²_F
paraphrase: Layer-wise AA-SVD objective: anchor target to original output W X, condition on shifted input X'.
preconditions: X = original calibration activations into W; X' = activations into W in the partially compressed model on the same calibration samples.
consequences: Apply Theorem 3.2 with A=X, B=X'.

verbatim: The solution operates only on the covariance matrices XX'^T and X'X'^T, not on raw activations, so its cost is independent of the number of calibration tokens.
paraphrase: AA-SVD requires only the cross-covariance XX'^T and shifted-input covariance X'X'^T; raw activations need not be retained — accumulate covariances in a streaming fashion.
preconditions: Forward passes available for both original and partially compressed networks on the calibration set.
consequences: Memory cost is O(n²) per layer regardless of token count; key implementation hint for Stage 3.

label: Algorithm 1 signature
verbatim: Require: Weight matrix W ∈ R^{m×n}, original inputs X ∈ R^{n×l}, shifted inputs X' ∈ R^{n×l}, target rank k
paraphrase: CompressLayer takes weight, original inputs, shifted inputs, and target rank.
consequences: n/a

label: Algorithm 1 step 1
verbatim: 1: Set A = X, B = X' {shift-aware: A=B=X'; input-aware: A=B=X}
paraphrase: Default AA-SVD = anchored-adaptive (A=X, B=X'). Same algorithm covers shift-aware (A=B=X') and input-aware (A=B=X) variants by parameter choice.
consequences: Stage 3 selects A=X, B=X' to instantiate AA-SVD.

label: Algorithm 1 step 2
verbatim: 2: Compute C = A B^T and S = B B^T
paraphrase: Compute cross-covariance C = X X'^T and shifted-input covariance S = X' X'^T.
consequences: Two covariance matrices are the only state needed downstream.

label: Algorithm 1 step 3
verbatim: 3: Factorize: S = R R^T {e.g. Cholesky or EVD}
paraphrase: Factorize the shifted covariance S = X'X'^T as R R^T (Cholesky or eigendecomposition acceptable).
preconditions: S positive definite (rank-deficient case → Tikhonov ε I).
consequences: R plays the role of L_B in Theorem 3.2.

label: Algorithm 1 step 4
verbatim: 4: Compute M = W C S^{-1} R
paraphrase: Form whitened cross-projection M = W · (X X'^T) · (X' X'^T)^{-1} · R.
consequences: M is the matrix whose rank-k SVD truncation gives the optimum.

label: Algorithm 1 step 5
verbatim: 5: Truncated SVD: [U_k, Σ_k, V_k] = SVD_k(M)
paraphrase: Take rank-k truncated SVD of M.
consequences: n/a

label: Algorithm 1 step 6 (return)
verbatim: 6: return factorized weight U = U_k Σ_k, V = R^{-T} V_k, so that W' = U V^T
paraphrase: De-whiten: U-factor absorbs Σ_k; V-factor is V_k right-multiplied by R^{-T}. Final compressed W' = U V^T = U_k Σ_k V_k^T R^{-1}.
consequences: Defines exact storage of compressed factors; this is the precise reconstruction formula Stage 3 must produce.

label: Algorithm 2 step 1
verbatim: 1: Extract input activations X ← X' ← E(calibration data) from the embedding layer E
paraphrase: Initialize both X and X' to the embedding-layer outputs (no upstream compression yet, so they coincide).
consequences: n/a

label: Algorithm 2 steps 4–7
verbatim: 4: for each linear layer W_j in L'_i do 5: Collect X_j from L_i and X'_j from L'_i by forward pass up to layer j 6: [U_j, V_j] ← CompressLayer(W_j, X_j, X'_j, k) 7: Update W_j ← U_j V_j^T in L'_i
paraphrase: Within a block, compress each linear layer in topological order: re-collect X_j (original block) and X'_j (compressed block-in-progress) by forward pass, run CompressLayer, install U_j V_j^T.
preconditions: Sequential per-layer order required (because X'_j depends on already-compressed predecessors).
consequences: Spec's Stage 3 must walk Q→K→V→O and gate→up→down in order.

label: Algorithm 2 step 9
verbatim: 9: Block-level refinement: optimize {U_j, V_j} and block-local parameters θ_i (e.g. norms, biases) jointly to minimize MSE(L_i(X), L'_i(X'))
paraphrase: After all linear layers in block i are compressed, jointly fine-tune the U/V factors AND block-local parameters (LayerNorm scales/biases, biases) to minimize block-output MSE between original block on X and compressed block on X'.
preconditions: All linear layers in block compressed; block-output MSE differentiable through factors.
consequences: θ_i ⊃ {LN scales, biases} is in the trainable set — this is non-trivial for Stage 3.

label: Algorithm 2 step 10
verbatim: 10: Update inputs for next block: X ← L_i(X), X' ← L'_i(X')
paraphrase: After block i finishes, advance both streams: X = original-block output on X; X' = compressed-block output on X'. Both propagate forward in lock-step.
preconditions: Block i fully compressed and refined.
consequences: Defines the dual-stream propagation that distinguishes AA-SVD from purely shift-aware methods.

label: §3.3 block objective
verbatim: min_{{U_j, V_j}, θ_i} E_{X∼D_i} ‖L_i(X) − L'_i(X')‖²_F
paraphrase: Block-level objective: expected Frobenius MSE between original block on original input X and compressed block on shifted input X', minimized over factor pairs and block-local trainable parameters θ_i.
preconditions: D_i = distribution of inputs to block i induced by calibration data.
consequences: Defines what Stage 3 block-level refinement must optimize.

verbatim: θ_i denotes the remaining trainable parameters of the block (e.g., normalization scales and biases). The optimization is thus over all factorized weights and block-local parameters jointly.
paraphrase: Block-local trainables include LayerNorm/RMSNorm scales and biases — these are tuned together with U/V.
consequences: Spec must mark norm scales/biases trainable inside Stage-3 refinement.

label: Calibration size
verbatim: we follow prior work and use 256 samples drawn from the WikiText2 (Merity et al., 2016) training split unless otherwise stated
paraphrase: Default calibration: 256 samples from WikiText2 train.
consequences: Stage 3 default calibration budget.

label: Calibration sequence length
verbatim: in our setting with 256 samples of length 2048, corresponding to over half a million effective columns
paraphrase: Calibration sequence length = 2048 tokens; 256 × 2048 ≈ 524k columns of activations.
consequences: Stage 3 calibration spec: 256 × 2048.

label: §4.3 calibration ablation
verbatim: perplexity drops sharply with the first ∼64 samples and largely saturates thereafter at both ratios ... Accuracy, by contrast, continues to improve substantially beyond 64 samples—particularly at ratio 0.8, where accuracy rises steeply between 64 and 128 samples before gradually plateauing
paraphrase: PPL plateaus by ~64 calibration samples; downstream accuracy keeps improving up to 128–256, hence the choice of 256.
consequences: Justifies 256 default; smaller calibrations acceptable for PPL-only audits.

label: Block refinement optimizer
verbatim: we use the AdamW optimizer (Loshchilov & Hutter, 2017) with a learning rate of 10^{-4}, trained for 25 epochs over the calibration data with a cosine learning rate schedule and linear warmup, with a batch size of 32
paraphrase: Block-refine training config: AdamW, lr=1e-4, 25 epochs, cosine schedule, linear warmup, batch size 32.
consequences: These are the exact hyperparameters Stage 3 must adopt for block refinement to be paper-faithful.

label: §B.2
verbatim: these forward passes can be executed in batches on GPU with the resulting activations offloaded to CPU memory between blocks, keeping peak GPU memory usage bounded
paraphrase: Implementation hint: offload X / X' tensors to CPU between blocks to bound GPU memory.
consequences: Recommended Stage 3 memory strategy.

label: §B.1
verbatim: multiple linear layers can share the same covariance matrix if they operate on the same input distribution (e.g. query, key and value projections or MLP gate and up projections within the same block), so the covariance can be reused across layers to amortize the cost
paraphrase: Q/K/V share input covariance; gate/up share input covariance — compute once per group.
consequences: Stage 3 optimization: 2 covariance computations per block (attn-input and MLP-input) instead of 7.

label: §B.1
verbatim: covariance matrices being computed in Step 2 can be implemented efficiently in batches and by additively accumulating the outer products (XX^T, XX'^T and X'X'^T), without explicitly materializing the full activation matrices
paraphrase: Stream covariances with running outer-product accumulation; never materialize full X / X' tensors.
consequences: Bounds memory by hidden dim², independent of token count.

label: §B.3 compression ratio
verbatim: ρ = (mk + nk) / (mn)
paraphrase: Effective compression ratio for rank-k factorization of m×n matrix is ρ = k(m+n)/(mn). E.g. m=n=4096, k=512 → ρ=0.125, 4× reduction.
consequences: Maps spec's ratio target to per-layer rank k.

label: §B.3
verbatim: Since attention projections are compressed, the activations stored in the cache scale with k rather than n, yielding proportional savings in both memory and bandwidth.
paraphrase: Compressing attention projections automatically shrinks the KV cache from O(n) to O(k) per token.
consequences: Inference-time memory benefit beyond parameter reduction.

label: §B.4 Dobi-SVD remapping
verbatim: Dobi-SVD (Wang et al., 2025a) proposes a remapping that stores the smaller factor and the top min(m, n) rows/columns of the larger factor in half precision (16-bit → 8-bit), with the remaining (max(m, n) − min(m, n)) rows/columns kept in full precision. ... ρ = max(m, n)·k/(mn) = k/min(m, n)
paraphrase: Optional Dobi-SVD-style remapping: half-precision storage for the smaller factor + top min(m,n) of larger factor; redefines ρ = k/min(m,n) so all ρ ∈ [0,1] map to k = ρ·min(m,n).
consequences: AA-SVDq reports ratios under this convention for fair comparison with Dobi-SVD.

label: §4.3 Table 5
verbatim: Refinement consistently and substantially improves all objectives: most strikingly, it rescues the input-agnostic objective from degeneracy and lifts the input-aware objective to the best overall performance at both ratios—6.89 PPL and 0.50 accuracy at ratio 0.8, and 8.35 PPL at ratio 0.6—establishing that block-level joint optimization is the dominant factor driving final quality.
paraphrase: Block-level refinement is THE dominant quality driver; with refinement enabled, the input-aware (‖WX − W'X‖²_F) objective yields the BEST final results — even better than the anchored-adaptive objective.
preconditions: All four objectives + refinement compared on LLaMA-7B at ρ=0.8 and 0.6.
consequences: Critical: paper recommends pairing input-aware initialization with block-level refinement, NOT the anchored-adaptive layer objective alone.

label: §4.3 conclusion
verbatim: Accordingly, our method pairs the input-aware objective with block-level refinement.
paraphrase: Final recommended AA-SVD recipe: input-aware layer objective ‖WX − W'X‖²_F (Corollary 3.3 with B=X) + block-level joint refinement.
consequences: Spec must NOT use the anchored-adaptive layer objective alone; must use input-aware whitening + block refinement to be paper-faithful.

label: Table 5 row
paraphrase: Input-agnostic at ρ=0.8: degenerate (PPL 2e4) without refinement; rescued to PPL 7.35, acc 0.50 with refinement.
consequences: Refinement alone can rescue even the worst initialization.

label: Table 5 row
paraphrase: Input-aware at ρ=0.8: PPL 7.89→6.89 with refinement (best overall).
consequences: Best objective is input-aware + refinement.

label: Table 5 row
paraphrase: Shift-aware at ρ=0.8: PPL 8.22→7.28 with refinement; does not surpass input-aware after refinement.
consequences: Shift-anchored loses to input-aware once refinement is applied.

label: Table 5 row
paraphrase: Anchored-adaptive at ρ=0.8: PPL 7.68→7.08 with refinement. Best WITHOUT refinement, second-best WITH refinement.
consequences: Anchored-adaptive shines without refinement; loses slightly to input-aware after refinement.

label: Table 1
paraphrase: LLaMA-7B ρ=0.8 AA-SVD: Wiki2=6.89, PTB=12.30, C4=12.04; avg-acc=0.50; -8.9% drop.
consequences: Headline number for ρ=0.8.

label: Table 1
consequences: n/a

label: Table 1
consequences: n/a

label: Table 1
paraphrase: With Dobi-style remapping at nominal ρ=0.4 (effective k = ρ·min(m,n)), AA-SVDq achieves Wiki2=8.61, avg-acc=0.44.
consequences: Dramatic improvement vs. AA-SVD at same nominal ratio.

label: Table 2
verbatim: 0.8 AA-SVD LLaMA-2-7B PPL 6.84 Acc 0.50 ; 0.6 AA-SVD LLaMA-2-7B PPL 8.55 Acc 0.44
consequences: Cross-architecture validation.

label: Table 2
verbatim: 0.8 AA-SVD LLaMA-3-8B PPL 9.58 Acc 0.50 ; 0.6 AA-SVD LLaMA-3-8B PPL 13.66 Acc 0.41
consequences: n/a

label: Table 2
verbatim: 0.8 AA-SVD Qwen-2.5-7B PPL 8.53 Acc 0.53 ; 0.6 AA-SVD Qwen-2.5-7B PPL 11.00 Acc 0.44
paraphrase: Qwen-2.5-7B AA-SVD: ρ=0.8 → 8.53/0.53; ρ=0.6 → 11.00/0.44. Stage 3's primary target architecture family.
consequences: Qwen baseline numbers for spec validation.

label: Table 4
paraphrase: LLaMA-7B WikiText2 PPL under fixed memory budgets: 10GB→6.89, 9GB→7.14, 8GB→7.84, 7GB→8.35; AA-SVD wins at every budget vs LLM-Pruner/SliceGPT/BlockPruner/SAES-SVD.
consequences: Practical deployment-tier numbers.

label: §5 Limitations
verbatim: Our current method applies a uniform compression ratio across all layers, with per-layer ranks derived from that ratio rather than optimized individually.
paraphrase: AA-SVD uses a single global compression ratio ρ; per-layer rank k_j = ρ·(m_j n_j/(m_j+n_j)) [or k = ρ·min(m,n) under remapping]. No per-layer rank search.
consequences: Spec D7a per-projection rank-bias is paper-EXTERNAL; AA-SVD itself has no per-projection bias.

verbatim: sensitivity-based approaches evaluate each layer in isolation, but our final compressed factors emerge from block-level joint refinement, making per-layer sensitivity a poor proxy for the true cost of reducing a layer's capacity.
paraphrase: Per-layer sensitivity scoring is fundamentally mismatched with block-level refinement, since refinement re-allocates capacity across layers within a block.
consequences: Warning against importing ASVD/STRS-style rank search into Stage 3.

label: §3.1 / Alg.2
verbatim: X' the corresponding shifted activations—collected by running the same calibration samples through the partially compressed network up to (but not including) the current component
paraphrase: X' is computed by running calibration samples through the partially compressed network up to (but not including) the current component. AA-SVD requires maintaining BOTH the original-network forward stream (for X) and the compressed-network forward stream (for X') in lock-step.
consequences: Stage 3 must keep two forward passes (original + compressed-so-far) on identical calibration batches.

verbatim: This objective enforces that the compressed outputs W'X' stay close to the original outputs W X, anchoring the compressed network to the behavior of the uncompressed one while simultaneously adapting to the shifted input distribution.
paraphrase: 'Anchor' = the original output W X (computed from the uncompressed network's activations). 'Adaptive' = conditioning the regression on the shifted input X' (from the compressed network).
consequences: Resolves any spec ambiguity about what 'anchored' refers to: it is the OUTPUT side, not the input side.

label: §2 shift-aware
verbatim: when upstream compression has degraded representations, anchoring solely to X' risks amplifying divergence from the original network's behavior; moreover, X' is estimated from a finite calibration batch and may be noisy, introducing instability.
paraphrase: Pure shift-aware (Dobi-SVD style, A=B=X') has two failure modes: (i) drifts away from original network, (ii) noisy on finite calibration → instability.
consequences: Why AA-SVD anchors to W X rather than W X'.

label: §2 input-aware
verbatim: as layers are compressed sequentially, the actual inputs received by each sub-module increasingly diverge from X — and since input-aware methods do not account for this error accumulation, the compressed model's behavior can diverge substantially from the original.
paraphrase: Pure input-aware (SVD-LLM, A=B=X) ignores upstream-induced distribution shift, so error accumulates with depth.
consequences: Motivates either AA-SVD layer objective OR (per §4.3) block-level refinement to absorb the shift.

verbatim: factorization yields dense, structured factors—enabling the commutation (UV^T)X = U(V^T X)—that integrate seamlessly with standard linear algebra libraries
paraphrase: Compressed forward pass exploits associativity: (UV^T)X = U(V^T X), reducing FLOPs to O(mk + nk).
preconditions: Compressed weight stored as U V^T.
consequences: Inference-time speedup mechanism.

label: §4 / Table 2
verbatim: we compress models from the LLaMA (Touvron et al., 2023) and Qwen (Bai et al., 2023) families ... LLaMA-2-7B, LLaMA-2-13B, LLaMA-3-1B, LLaMA-3-8B, Qwen-2.5-7B
paraphrase: Evaluation suite: LLaMA-7B, LLaMA-2-7B, LLaMA-2-13B, LLaMA-3-1B, LLaMA-3-8B, Qwen-2.5-7B.
consequences: Confirms Qwen-2.5-7B is in-distribution for AA-SVD; supports MoE-Qwen application via Stage 3.

verbatim: Winogrande (Sakaguchi et al., 2020), PIQA (Bisk et al., 2020), ARC-Easy and ARC-Challenge (Clark et al., 2018), OpenBookQA (Mihaylov et al., 2018), HellaSwag (Zellers et al., 2019) and MathQA (Amini et al., 2019)
paraphrase: Seven zero-shot reasoning benchmarks: WinoGrande, PIQA, ARC-Easy, ARC-Challenge, OpenBookQA, HellaSwag, MathQA.
consequences: Spec evaluation harness should include these for paper-faithful comparison.

verbatim: language modeling perplexity on WikiText2, C4 (Raffel et al., 2020), and PTB (Marcinkiewicz, 1994)
paraphrase: PPL benchmarks: WikiText2, C4, PTB.
consequences: n/a

label: Figure 4 / §4.3
verbatim: AA-SVD achieves the lowest error throughout the network on both metrics: cosine distance rises gradually to ∼0.1–0.15 and then plateaus in later layers, while MSE remains consistently below SVD-LLM across all layer indices.
paraphrase: AA-SVD's intermediate-feature MSE and cosine distance plateau by mid-network at ~0.1–0.15 cosine; SVD-LLM grows monotonically with depth-spikes; naive SVD saturates near 1.0 cosine immediately.
preconditions: LLaMA-7B at ρ=0.8 evaluated on held-out WikiText2 test split.
consequences: Empirical evidence of error-accumulation suppression.

label: Table 5 row
paraphrase: Input-agnostic at ρ=0.6: PPL 5e5 → 10.93 with refinement (rescued from total degeneracy).
consequences: n/a

label: Table 5 row
paraphrase: Input-aware ρ=0.6: 13.11 → 8.35 with refinement (best overall).
consequences: n/a

label: Table 5 row
paraphrase: Anchored-adaptive ρ=0.6: 12.19 → 8.52 with refinement.
consequences: n/a

label: §6 Conclusion
verbatim: we proposed an anchored and adaptive layer-wise compression objective with a closed-form solution, efficient to compute with a small calibration set. Second, and more critically, we introduced block-level refinement
paraphrase: Two contributions, in order of importance: (1) anchored-adaptive layer-wise objective; (2) block-level refinement (declared 'more critical').
consequences: Authors themselves rate block-refinement as the dominant contribution — confirming Stage 3 must include it.
